sources: SF Next_Step__c history (no quantified pain captured)
Lack of direct PIF stakeholder discovery call
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been articulated by PIF across any captured interaction. The Next Step history from 8 Oct 2025 indicates PIF will run a 'formal exercise for them only (i.e. not the PIF companies) in 2026' covering 'whole S2P market,' implying a broad procurement transformation scope, but no specific pain points, cost reduction targets, cycle-time improvements, or error-rate baselines have been captured. The deal has been vendor-gated through Efficio (consultant-led RFI/RFP process) since inception, and no direct discovery call with PIF stakeholders has occurred to surface quantified pain. Without a quantified value case, Zycus has no anchor for pricing justification, ROI defense, or economic-buyer sponsorship. The deal is being run as a compliance exercise (respond to RFI/RFP) rather than a value-led conversation. Quantifying the business problem with the relevant stakeholder (Abdulaziz Alsuwailem) or procurement leadership is the critical missing step.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 2 Dec 2025, 13 May 2026, 20 Mar 2026, 18 Jun 2026
Lack of PIF internal champion evidence
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The primary contact throughout the cycle has been Andrew Waterhouse (Efficio consultant), who is a process gatekeeper, not a champion. The 2 Dec 2025 Next Step documents an attempt to build a relationship: 'Invited Andrew to the relevant stakeholder - response received, he appreciated the invite but unlikely can make it, will catch up in the new year with a progress update,' and the 13 May 2026 entry notes 'Had some back and forth with Andrew after inviting him to the Summit - he confirmed that there is no update on the RFP yet.' Andrew is responsive but has provided no advocacy, no internal coaching, and no process acceleration. He is vendor-agnostic per Efficio's mandate (20 Mar 2026: 'not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic'). The PIF-side contacts (Abdullah Almanyah, Lujain Mosaeb, SRM alias) are listed as OpportunityContactRoles but have no documented engagement history, advocacy evidence, or access to the economic buyer. Sofian Ouahchi (Efficio Senior Digital Consultant, Evaluator role) is another consultant, not a PIF internal advocate. The 18 Jun 2026 Next Step indicates the owner is attempting to reconnect and escalate to Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP, economic buyer) via BD partner intro, but this has not yet materialized. With 24 days to close, no internal champion, and all access mediated by a vendor-agnostic consultant, Zycus has no voice inside PIF and no ability to shape, accelerate, or de-risk the process.

competition.narrative: Zycus is competing in a crowded formal RFP process against SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, JAGGAER, and potentially other vendors (per Competitors__c field 'Others'). The 8 Oct 2025 Next Step notes the exercise 'will involve whole S2P market,' confirming a broad competitive field. SAP Ariba is the likely primary competitor given PIF's enterprise scale, regional presence requirements, and the typical KSA market preference for SAP-integrated solutions. GEP Worldwide is a strong regional player with KSA presence and may have an advantage on local-hosting and category-management capabilities. The 20 Mar 2026 Next Step documents Zycus's attempt to leverage Efficio for competitive intelligence: 'Had alliances reach out to Efficio to see if we can be influential, under the guise of a wider international partnership, politely responded not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic.' This confirms Zycus has no competitive visibility, no ability to shape RFP criteria in its favor, and no insight into how competitors are positioned. Zycus's wedge (Merlin AI, modern UX, configurability) has not been landed with PIF stakeholders because no discovery or demo has occurred. The Products field includes Merlin and full S2P suite, but whether AI is a decision criterion or a differentiator is unknown. Without a champion, EB access, or process influence, Zycus is in a reactive, score-the-RFP posture with no competitive angle to activate.

sources: Lack of PIF stakeholder discovery calls
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by PIF stakeholders. The 8 Oct 2025 Next Step describes the opportunity as a 'formal exercise for them only (i.e. not the PIF companies) in 2026' covering the 'whole S2P market,' indicating a planned procurement-platform consolidation or modernization, but the WHY (current-state pain, system gaps, cost/risk drivers) is undocumented. The 10 Sep 2025 Next Step notes Efficio reached out about a 'high-level KSA opportunity in 2026,' implying Efficio identified a procurement transformation need, but the pain owner (procurement head, CFO, SVP) and the pain specifics (legacy ERP limitations, manual processes, supplier risk, spend visibility gaps) have not been captured. Without direct discovery calls with PIF stakeholders, Zycus has no line of sight to the compelling event, urgency, or consequences of inaction. The deal is being run as a reactive RFP response rather than a pain-led, solution-designed engagement. The absence of a pain owner and quantified pain makes it impossible to build urgency, differentiate on value, or secure executive sponsorship.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 12 Jan 2026, 22 Jan 2026, 8 Oct 2025, 18 Jun 2026
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have been partially initiated but not advanced. The 12 Jan 2026 Next Step documents 'Information request, NDA, Code of conduct etc. - subsequently submitted,' and the 22 Jan 2026 Next Step notes 'Further info requested - liaising with Legal, this is a precursor to the formal RFI.' This indicates PIF required vendor registration, NDA, and legal compliance documentation as a prerequisite to RFI participation, and Zycus completed this. However, no MSA, SOW, or commercial contracting activity has commenced because the RFP has not been issued. The 8 Oct 2025 Next Step flags a critical paper-process blocker: PIF 'will require KSA hosting and will involve whole S2P market,' and later notes 'suggested we might want to invest in local hosting earlier.' This KSA local-hosting requirement is a potential disqualifier if Zycus cannot demonstrate a KSA legal entity, regional office, or partnership that satisfies PIF's sovereignty and data-residency mandate. The Next Step field includes an open action 'confirm KSA local-hosting commitment & provisional timeline,' indicating this remains unresolved. No SI/partner role has been formalized, though the 18 Jun 2026 Next Step references Brien Rodrigues (BD) as an intro channel, suggesting informal partner exploration but no locked engagement model.

sources: SF Next_Step__c history (all engagement via Efficio or lower-level PIF contacts)
economic_buyer.narrative: Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP) is named in the Next Step field as 'Economic Buyer' as of 18 Jun 2026, but there is no evidence of direct engagement with him. The 18 Jun 2026 Next Step entry states the owner 'draft email and shared it with BD (Brien Rodrigues) as Intro for me to reconnect as no progress from customer, this email to setup new meeting with Abdulaziz Alsuwailem (SVP / Economic Buyer) and including all team from PIF,' indicating outreach was INITIATED but not yet completed. No prior calls, emails, or meetings with Alsuwailem are captured in the deal history. All documented engagement has been with Andrew Waterhouse (Efficio consultant, process gatekeeper) and lower-level PIF contacts (Abdullah Almanyah, Lujain Mosaeb, SRM alias). The economic buyer has not been in the room for any discovery, demo, or commercial conversation, and his sponsorship level (active vs. passive) is unknown. Without direct EB engagement, budget authority, urgency, and commercial approval path remain unconfirmed.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 2 Mar 2026, 15 Mar 2026, 7 Feb 2026, 8 Oct 2025, 20 Mar 2026, 13 Apr 2026, 13 May 2026
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is consultant-led (Efficio) with a multi-stage formal procurement exercise: RFI (received 2 Mar 2026, submitted 15 Mar 2026), followed by formal RFP (anticipated 'after Eid, end March/April 2026' per 7 Feb 2026 Next Step, still not issued as of 13 May 2026), vendor shortlisting, demos, and final selection. The 8 Oct 2025 Next Step indicates the exercise is 'for them only (i.e. not the PIF companies),' meaning PIF central procurement is the buyer, not a portfolio company. The process is gated by Efficio, and the 20 Mar 2026 Next Step confirms Efficio declined to be influenced ('not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic'), meaning Zycus has no process lever or early-stage shaping opportunity. No multi-tier approval chain or committee structure has been documented. The economic buyer (Alsuwailem, SVP) and decision-maker roles are not yet mapped into the process stages. The timeline has slipped repeatedly: RFI was due 'after Eid (end March)' but arrived early (2 Mar 2026); RFP was expected April 2026 but remains unissued as of 13 May 2026 with 'no update.' The lack of RFP issuance and the consultant-gated process with no internal champion or EB access make the process opaque and uncontrollable for Zycus.

decision_criteria.narrative: The RFI was submitted 15 Mar 2026 per Next Step, indicating PIF has documented requirements, but the specific evaluation criteria, weighting, and scoring rubric have not been shared with the relevant stakeholder 8 Oct 2025 Next Step notes the exercise 'will involve whole S2P market,' suggesting a comprehensive capability evaluation across source-to-pay modules, but no detail on must-haves, differentiators, or scoring methodology. The Products field lists full S2P suite plus Merlin (AI), implying broad scope, but whether AI/Merlin is a weighted criterion or a nice-to-have is unknown. The Competitors field names Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, JAGGAER, and Others, indicating a crowded competitive set, but Zycus has no intel on how PIF is scoring vendors or which capabilities are decision-critical. The 20 Mar 2026 Next Step documents that Efficio (the RFP gatekeeper) 'politely responded not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic' when Zycus attempted to gain insight into the process, confirming Zycus has no visibility into or influence over the decision criteria. Without access to the RFP scoring framework or decision-maker priorities, Zycus is flying blind into a multi-vendor competitive evaluation.

items.name: Andy shuts down Brien's follow-up, says no review needed now
items.summary: Responding to Brien's request for a 30-min working session on PIF's S2C initiative, Andy Waterhouse told him to stop copying departed PIF staff and route all communication through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him, adding that PIF does not need further review of the Zycus offering at this time — a clear stall signal on the deal.

items.name: Carl apologizes and confirms single-channel comms
items.summary: Carl Kimball quickly responded to Andy's rebuke, apologizing and confirming that all communications will now go through Hakim and himself, and that the internal team has been updated — an attempt to repair the relationship and unblock further engagement.

items.name: Andy acknowledges Carl's correction
items.summary: Andy sent a brief one-line reply thanking Carl for confirming the consolidated communication channel, closing the loop on the process issue for now.

items.name: Andy resurfaces after holiday, asks for single channel and KAFD visit
items.summary: In an earlier reply on the same thread, Andy explained he'd been on holiday, noted he was also getting separate emails from Brien Rodrigues and asked Carl to consolidate to one channel, while inviting Carl to propose dates to meet in person at KAFD — the root cause of the day's channel-cleanup exchange.

day_summary.overall: A messy multi-thread email exchange forced a hard reset on how Zycus engages PIF: Andy Waterhouse pushed back on Brien's outreach, pointing out several cc'd contacts have left PIF and stating there's no need for further review of the Zycus offering right now, while insisting all communication be consolidated through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him directly. Carl quickly conceded the point and confirmed the team will now route everything through Hakim and himself, and separately worked to set up an in-person meeting with Andy in KAFD. Net effect: the deal's active review is paused/stalled per the buyer, and the priority has shifted from pitching capabilities to first fixing the channel and re-earning a working session.

Efficio is vendor-agnostic gatekeeper running the RFP process and explicitly stated on 20 March 2026 they are not interested in being influenced, limiting Zycus's ability to shape the process or criteria.
No direct engagement with the economic buyer (Abdulaziz Alsuwailem, SVP) or senior PIF stakeholders; owner is attempting to reconnect via partner as of 18 June 2026.

RFP issuance has been pending since 13 April 2026 with no movement as of 13 May; PIF has not provided a timeline for the formal RFP, blocking all forward progress and making the 31 July close date unachievable.
Significant product gaps in market intelligence and category management per MEDDPICC Products Considered field may expose Zycus in the RFP scoring if PIF weights these capabilities heavily.

Secure direct economic buyer engagement before RFP shortlist: meeting with the relevant stakeholder (SVP/EB named 18 June) to validate the AI automation business case and gain active sponsorship, bypassing the Efficio gatekeeper where possible.

Proactively resolve the KSA local-hosting blocker as a competitive differentiator: confirm Zycus's local-hosting commitment and timeline (or EY partnership model) and surface this in the RFP response and demo workshops, as many global vendors may struggle with this requirement.

Position Merlin AI and agentic automation as the core wedge in a crowded MQ Leader field: ensure the demo workshops and RFP response lead with AI-driven sourcing, contract intelligence, and intake automation to differentiate from traditional workflow platforms like Coupa and SAP Ariba.
Andy Waterhouse
Supplier Relationship Management
